commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Telegram dispatch/test coverage and an opt-in live proof harness so an in-flight streamed answer preamble is preserved as its own message when a tool boundary occurs mid-stream.

PR surface: Source +25, Tests +191, Docs +42, Other +335. Total +593 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The PR body provides live fast/delayed Bot API output, and source inspection shows current main has finalized-lane and rendered-tool-progress rotation paths but lacks this unfinalized suppressed-progress tool-boundary rotation.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is a narrow mechanical repair: preserve the live-race rotation while avoiding below-debounce never-sent drafts.

target.title: fix(telegram): rotate in-flight answer preamble at tool boundary
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes a Telegram bug where a streamed answer **preamble** is overwritten instead of preserved when a tool runs mid-stream. The model streams a short preamble (e.g. *"Let me check the current state of that file."*), calls a tool, then resumes with post-tool text — and the post-tool text **edited the preamble bubble** instead of sending a new message, so earlier content vanished from the chat. (Voice/media were unaffected — a separate code path.)
- Why now: this hits default Telegram deployments (partial streaming is the default) and silently drops assistant output mid-turn.
- Root cause is an **async race** at the tool boundary in the streaming draft pipeline (`dispatchTelegramMessage` + `createTelegramDraftStream`): rotation to a new message was gated on the preamble's Bot API send already having completed. Under fast timing the tool boundary fires while that send is still in flight, the guard bails, and the resumed text edits the preamble.
- Intended outcome: the preamble always becomes its own message and post-tool text starts a fresh one, at any timing.
- Out of scope: non-streaming and "progress" streaming modes, and media/voice replies (separate, already-correct paths).
- Reviewers should focus on `rotateInFlightAnswerPreambleForToolBoundary` and the await-then-rotate ordering in `rotateLaneForNewMessage` → `stream.stop()` → `loop.flush()`.

### Fix

`rotateInFlightAnswerPreambleForToolBoundary` now rotates on **intent** — a preamble partial was emitted (`hasStreamedMessage`) — not on send-ack. It no longer requires `stream.messageId()` to be a number. `rotateAnswerLaneForNewMessage → rotateLaneForNewMessage → stream.stop() → loop.flush()` **awaits the in-flight preamble send** (`draft-stream-loop.ts`: `if (inFlightPromise) await inFlightPromise`) before `forceNewMessage()`, so the preamble is committed to its own message and the resumed text starts fresh — it can never fall through to an edit.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #88682, Related #80294

This is the pre-existing near-miss to call out:

- **#88682** ("Preserve Telegram streamed text blocks between tool calls") added `rotateFinalizedStream`, which rotates across a tool boundary **only for a lane already finalized** (`if (!lane.stream || !lane.finalized) return;`). It fixed the finalized-preamble case and stopped short of the in-flight (unfinalized) preamble.
- **#80294** ("keep verbose tool results separate from final answers") added `prepareAnswerLaneForToolProgress`, which rotates **only when tool progress actually renders** — suppressed in partial streaming.
- The first commit of this PR added a tool-boundary rotation but gated it on send-ack, so it fixed only the slow path; the follow-up commit corrects the async race by rotating on intent. The reported bug was raised privately by @csillag with a live Telegram client; there is no public issue.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram in-flight answer preamble was overwritten by post-tool text (the bot edited its previous message instead of sending a new one) when a tool runs mid-stream.
- Real environment tested: Live Telegram Bot API — a dedicated throwaway bot posting to a private chat (real network, real message ids), driven through the real `dispatchTelegramMessage` + `createTelegramDraftStream` by the opt-in harness `proof/telegram-edit-vs-send-proof.ts`. Token and chat id redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```
  # fast: tool fires while the preamble send is still in flight (the failing case)
  TG_BOT_TOKEN=<redacted> TG_CHAT_ID=<redacted> TOOL_DELAY_MS=0 \
    node --import tsx proof/telegram-edit-vs-send-proof.ts postfix-fast
  # delayed (~2.5s): preamble send acks first
  TG_BOT_TOKEN=<redacted> TG_CHAT_ID=<redacted> TOOL_DELAY_MS=2500 \
    node --import tsx proof/telegram-edit-vs-send-proof.ts postfix-delayed
  ```
- Evidence after fix (copied live Bot API output):
  ```
  # post-fix FAST
  sendMessage      message_id=7  text="Let me check the current state of that file."
  sendMessage      message_id=8  text="Done — the stale version was already removed earlier."
  SUMMARY sends=2 edits=0 preamblePreserved=true

  # post-fix DELAYED
  sendMessage      message_id=9   text="Let me check the current state of that file."
  sendMessage      message_id=10  text="Done — the stale version was already removed earlier."
  SUMMARY sends=2 edits=0 preamblePreserved=true
  ```
- Observed result after fix: two separate messages — the preamble is preserved and the post-tool text is a new message — at BOTH timings, fast included. Before the fix, the same live run (reverted tree, fast timing) produced one send plus one edit on the same message id, overwriting the preamble:
  ```
  # pre-fix FAST (control)
  sendMessage      message_id=11  text="Let me check the current state of that file."
  editMessageText  message_id=11  text="Done — the stale version was already removed earlier."
  SUMMARY sends=1 edits=1 preamblePreserved=false
  ```
- What was not tested: non-streaming and "progress" streaming modes, and media/voice replies (separate paths); load/concurrency beyond the reproduced turn.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: run against a throwaway bot in a private chat (token + chat id redacted). The harness replays the runtime's assistant-event sequence (preamble → tool → resume) deterministically, but drives the real dispatch + draft stream + live Bot API — not a mocked transport. It is opt-in and no-ops without a token, so it never runs in CI.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): see the "pre-fix FAST (control)" capture above.

## Tests and validation

- Commands: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram` (full Telegram suite), the targeted dispatch/lane suites, `pnpm tsgo:extensions` + `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`, plus the live harness commands above.
- Regression coverage: added dispatch-level real-stream coverage for the in-flight preamble across a tool boundary (plus a fresh-boundary control and the deliverer-level contract test), and the opt-in live harness for the async race a unit test cannot catch.
- What failed before: on the reverted tree the in-flight repro and the live fast-timing run overwrite the preamble (`sends=1 edits=1`).
- Supplemental unit/type results: targeted suites green (165); full Telegram suite 2085 pass with 3 unrelated pre-existing/flaky failures (2 sticker-description timeouts; 1 model-callback-list test that passes in isolation on both trees); typechecks clean. These are supplemental — the real proof is above.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — Telegram now sends a separate message for resumed post-tool text instead of overwriting the preamble.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No — only outbound message rotation logic.
- Highest-risk area: over-rotating could split a single logical message into two.
- How is that risk mitigated: rotation fires only when a preamble was actually streamed and not finalized, defers to the existing tool-progress rotation to avoid double-rotation, and is covered by unit tests plus live proof at both timings.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Waiting on: review. Draft status is managed by the author.
- Addressed: reworked the initial send-ack-gated fix after live proof exposed an async race (now rotates on intent), and added the opt-in live harness as durable real-behavior evidence.

---
*Authored by Sancho Panza, an AI squire acting on behalf of @csillag.*
target.author: sancho-csillag

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
